rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical tandem trifluoromethylation/cyclization of N-(but-3-en-1-yl)anilines with Togni's reagent II to give 4-(trifluoromethylmethyl)-1,2,3,4-tetrahydroquinolines
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of Togni's reagent II (facilitated by TFA, shifting reduction from -2.73 V to -1.65 V vs SCE) generates a CF3 radical; subsequent anodic oxidation of a cyclohexadienyl-type radical intermediate closes the catalytic cycle

key_intermediates.name: CF3 radical generated by cathodic reduction of Togni's reagent II
Carbon-centered radical I from CF3 addition to terminal alkene of N-(but-3-en-1-yl)aniline
Cyclohexadienyl radical II from intramolecular radical cyclization onto the arene
Cyclohexadienyl cation III after anodic oxidation of II, prior to rearomatizing deprotonation

electron_flow: Cathode reduces TFA-activated Togni's reagent II to release a CF3 radical, which adds to the alkene of the aniline substrate to give C-radical I; intramolecular radical addition onto the arene gives cyclohexadienyl radical II, which is oxidized at the anode to cation III and deprotonated to rearomatize, affording the tetrahydroquinoline product.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; no product 3a formed, indicating radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited; supports radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely inhibited, consistent with radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: no-current control
probe_reagents.observation: No product formed without applied current; confirms electrochemistry is essential

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, TFA and mixtures
probe_reagents.observation: 1a and TFA show no reduction peak; 2a reduces at -2.73 V vs SCE; 2a+TFA shows new reduction peak at -1.65 V vs SCE, showing TFA facilitates cathodic reduction of Togni's reagent II

notes: TFA plays a critical role by protonating/activating Togni's reagent II, lowering its reduction potential by ~1 V and enabling efficient cathodic CF3 radical generation under mild conditions. Mechanism is supported by radical trapping, current on/off, and CV but no DFT or KIE was reported.
